description: The dense yellow metal called gold is 'solai'. It resists corrosion and can be hammered or drawn into very thin forms. Rarity, price, and ceremonial standing come from human arrangements rather than from the metal noun.
articulatory_notes: A narrow tongue groove sends unvoiced 's' into rounded 'o'. Lateral 'l' releases stressed open 'a', followed by a clean hiatus into final high-front 'i'.
usage_notes: One gold sample can take optional 'themo'. Purity and provenance describe the metal; a currency system must supply any monetary role.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: a yellow metal used where colour, reflection, or surface matters
semantic_domains.creation: a corrosion-resistant metal worked into thin forms and coatings
